sources.title: VHA Directive 1160.06(1) Inpatient Mental Health Services
National Guidelines for a Behavioral Health Coordinated System of Crisis Care
Protocols to Reduce Seclusion in Inpatient Mental Health Units

small.name: Crisis receiving center
small.notes: Assessment, recliner observation, sensory rooms, staff base

space_library.name: Entry / Reception
notes: Reception with calm, trauma-informed arrival and controlled transition to clinical areas.

space_library.name: Waiting / Family Lounge
notes: Provide lower-stimulus waiting and family support rather than ED-like congestion.

space_library.name: Assessment / Intake
notes: Assessment/intake immediately downstream of reception; trauma-informed and observable.

space_library.name: Open Observation / Recliner Milieu
notes: SAMHSA-linked crisis stabilization models may use chairs/recliners in an open milieu instead of only private rooms; sightlines and staff observation matter.

space_library.name: Consult / Exam Room
notes: Behavioral health intake often needs a medically capable consult/exam room to differentiate behavioral from medical emergency.

space_library.name: Sensory / Comfort Room
notes: VA evidence review notes sensory modulation rooms may reduce seclusion events and forced medication; environment is part of treatment.

space_library.name: Seclusion / Protective Room
notes: Use only where program/regulatory model requires it; ligature resistance, observation, and anti-barricade details are specialized.

space_library.name: Patient Bedroom / Short Stay Room
notes: VA planning references commonly organize inpatient mental health units in 8, 12, 16, or 20-bed increments, with separate and locked sleeping rooms for women on mixed-gender units.

space_library.name: Staff Base / Observation
notes: Staff base must maintain observation to open milieu and secure support spaces.

space_library.name: Medication / Clean / Soiled / Nourishment
notes: Cluster wet and medication support for observation and patient rooms.

envelope_assembly.wall: robust insulated stud/CMU system with secure detailing
envelope_assembly.glazing: limited, tamper-resistant glazing with therapeutic daylight

plan_archetypes.notes: Observation, de-escalation, and secure but non-institutional environment are key.
